title: asperula glomerata subsp. glomerata
intro: Asperula glomerata subsp. glomerata, also known as round-leaved woodruff, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family (Rubiaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high content of iridoid glycosides and terpenoids. The medicinal properties of Asperula glomerata subsp. glomerata are said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses, and it is also used as an ingredient in some herbal remedies and perfumes.
